subject: math
title: Numbers 0–10
explanation: Let's learn numbers from 0 to 10! Numbers are everywhere — count your fingers, your toys, and the stars! 🌟
examples: If you have 3 apples 🍎🍎🍎, that's the number 3
funTip: Did you know? Zero means nothing at all! Like an empty cookie jar 🍪

subject: math
title: Numbers 10–20
explanation: Now let's go higher! Numbers from 10 to 20. After 10 comes 11, 12... all the way to 20!
funTip: A soccer team has 11 players! ⚽

subject: math
title: Numbers 20–100
explanation: Big numbers time! We count by tens: 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100! 🎉
funTip: If you save 1 coin every day, in 100 days you'll have 100 coins! 💰

subject: math
title: Numbers 100–1000
explanation: Let's explore even bigger numbers! Hundreds go 100, 200, 300... up to 1000!
funTip: A giraffe is about 500 cm tall! 🦒

subject: math
title: Addition: Adding Small Numbers
explanation: Addition means putting things together! When you have 2 apples and get 3 more, you have 2 + 3 = 5 apples! 🍎
funTip: Think of addition as collecting more stickers! 🌈

subject: math
title: Addition: Sums up to 20
explanation: Let's add bigger numbers! Remember: start from the bigger number and count up. 7 + 5 = 12!
funTip: Use your fingers to help count! No one will judge 😄

subject: math
title: Addition: Two-Digit Numbers
explanation: Adding two-digit numbers: add ones first, then tens. 23 + 14 = 37! Stack them to make it easy.
funTip: Break big problems into small ones. You're a math wizard! 🧙‍♂️

subject: math
title: Addition: Three-Digit Numbers
explanation: Adding three-digit numbers! Same trick: ones, tens, then hundreds. Don't forget to carry over!
funTip: Imagine you're stacking coins — ones on ones, tens on tens! 🪙

subject: math
title: Subtraction: Taking Away
explanation: Subtraction means taking away! If you have 5 candies and eat 2, you have 5 - 2 = 3 candies left! 🍬
funTip: Subtraction is like sharing with friends! How kind! 💝

subject: math
title: Subtraction: Numbers up to 20
explanation: Let's subtract bigger numbers! 15 - 8 = 7. Count backwards from 15, eight steps!
funTip: Counting backwards is like a rocket launch! 🚀 3, 2, 1... blast off!

subject: math
title: Subtraction: Two-Digit Numbers
explanation: Subtracting two-digit numbers: subtract ones, then tens. 45 - 23 = 22!
funTip: Think of it as going down stairs — one step at a time! 🪜

subject: math
title: Subtraction: With Borrowing
explanation: Sometimes we need to borrow! If ones column is too small, borrow from tens. 42 - 17 = 25!
funTip: Borrowing is like asking your neighbor for help. Teamwork! 🤝

subject: math
title: Multiplication: What Is It?
explanation: Multiplication is fast addition! 3 × 2 means 3 + 3 = 6. It's like groups! 3 groups of 2! 🎯
funTip: Multiplication makes counting super fast! Like a superhero! 🦸

subject: math
title: Times Table: 2
explanation: The 2 times table: everything doubles! 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12...
funTip: Pairs of socks! Every pair is ×2! 🧦🧦

subject: math
title: Times Table: 3
funTip: A triangle has 3 sides. 4 triangles = 12 sides! 📐

subject: math
title: Times Table: 4 and 5
explanation: 4 times table: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20... 5 times table: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25... notice the pattern!
funTip: 5 times table always ends in 0 or 5. Easy! 🖐️

subject: math
title: Times Table: 6 and 7
funTip: 7 days in a week! 2 weeks = 7 × 2 = 14 days! 📅

subject: math
title: Times Table: 8 and 9
explanation: 8 times table: 8, 16, 24, 32, 40... 9 times table has a magic trick — digits always add to 9!
funTip: 9 × any number: use the finger trick! Hold down finger #N and count! 🖐️✋

subject: math
title: Times Table: 10, 11, 12
explanation: 10 times: just add a zero! 11 times small numbers: repeat the digit! 12 is the last one!
funTip: You now know ALL times tables! You're a multiplication master! 👑

subject: math
title: Division: Sharing Equally
explanation: Division means sharing equally! 6 ÷ 2 = 3. If you share 6 cookies between 2 friends, each gets 3! 🍪
funTip: Division is multiplication backwards! If 3 × 2 = 6, then 6 ÷ 2 = 3! 🔄

subject: math
title: Division: Bigger Numbers
explanation: Dividing bigger numbers: 24 ÷ 6 = 4. Use your times tables to help!
funTip: Times tables are the key to division. They're best friends! 👫

subject: math
title: Division: With Remainders
explanation: Sometimes things don't share perfectly! 7 ÷ 2 = 3 remainder 1. The leftover is the remainder!
funTip: Remainders are like leftover pizza slices. Someone lucky gets extra! 🍕

subject: math
title: Fractions: What Are They?
explanation: A fraction is a part of a whole! 1/2 means one part out of two equal parts. Like cutting a pizza in half! 🍕
funTip: When you share a chocolate bar equally with a friend, you each get 1/2! 🍫

subject: math
title: Fractions: Comparing
explanation: Which is bigger: 1/2 or 1/4? Think about pizza: half a pizza is bigger than a quarter! 1/2 > 1/4
funTip: The bigger the bottom number, the smaller each piece! 🧩

subject: math
title: Fractions: Adding & Subtracting
explanation: To add fractions with the same bottom number: just add the tops! 1/4 + 2/4 = 3/4
funTip: Same bottom? Just add the tops! Easy-peasy! 🎂

subject: math
title: Decimals: Introduction
explanation: Decimals are another way to show parts! 0.5 = 1/2. The dot is called a decimal point!
funTip: Money uses decimals! $1.50 means one dollar and fifty cents! 💵

subject: math
title: Decimals: Adding & Subtracting
explanation: Adding decimals: line up the decimal points! 1.5 + 2.3 = 3.8
funTip: Keep the dots lined up like soldiers in a row! 🎖️

subject: math
title: Shapes: Circle, Square, Triangle
explanation: Shapes are everywhere! A circle is round 🔵, a square has 4 equal sides ⬜, a triangle has 3 sides 🔺
funTip: Look around your room — how many shapes can you find? 👀

subject: math
title: Shapes: Rectangle, Oval, Diamond
explanation: More shapes! A rectangle has 4 sides (2 long, 2 short). An oval is a stretched circle. A diamond is a tilted square!
funTip: A rectangle is like a lazy square that stretched out for a nap! 😴

subject: math
title: Shapes: Pentagon, Hexagon, Octagon
explanation: Penta = 5 sides, Hexa = 6 sides, Octa = 8 sides! A stop sign is an octagon!
funTip: Bees are amazing builders — honeycomb is made of hexagons! 🐝

subject: math
title: Perimeter & Area
explanation: Perimeter = distance around a shape. Area = space inside. Perimeter of a square = 4 × side!
funTip: Perimeter is like walking around a park. Area is the grass inside! 🌳

subject: math
title: Telling Time: Hours
explanation: A clock has 12 numbers. The short hand shows hours! When it points to 3, it's 3 o'clock! ⏰
funTip: The short hand is slow like a turtle 🐢, the long hand is fast like a rabbit 🐇!

subject: math
title: Telling Time: Minutes
explanation: The long hand shows minutes! It goes from 0 to 60. When it points at 6, it's 30 minutes (half past)!
funTip: 60 minutes = 1 hour. Time to play for 60 minutes! ⏳

subject: math
title: Time: AM/PM & Elapsed Time
explanation: AM is morning (midnight to noon). PM is afternoon/night. How long from 2:00 to 5:00? 3 hours!
funTip: AM = After Midnight, PM = Past Midday! 🌙☀️

subject: math
title: Money: Coins & Bills
explanation: Money helps us buy things! Coins are small and round. Bills are paper. Let's learn their values!
funTip: Save your coins in a piggy bank! 🐷

subject: math
title: Money: Making Change
explanation: If something costs 7 and you pay 10, your change is 10 - 7 = 3! Always count your change!
funTip: Shopkeepers are math experts! 🏪

subject: math
title: Word Problems: Addition Stories
explanation: Let's solve stories with math! Read carefully, find the numbers, and add them!
examples: Ali has 3 cats, he gets 2 more. How many? 3+2=5
Sara has 4 books and buys 3 more. How many? 4+3=7
funTip: Word problems are just math stories! You're the hero who solves them! 🦸‍♀️

subject: math
title: Word Problems: Mixed Operations
explanation: Some stories need subtraction or multiplication! Read carefully to choose the right operation.
examples: Ali had 10 candies, gave away 4. How many left? 10-4=6
funTip: Keywords: 'more/total'=add, 'left/fewer'=subtract, 'each/groups'=multiply! 🔑

subject: math
title: Word Problems: Multi-Step
explanation: Harder problems need more than one step! Break them down and solve one step at a time.
examples: Ali has 20 coins. He spends 8 and earns 5. How many? 20-8+5=17
funTip: Big problems are just small problems stacked together! 🧱

subject: math
title: Number Patterns
explanation: Patterns repeat! 2, 4, 6, 8 — what's next? 10! The pattern adds 2 each time!
funTip: Patterns are like secret codes — once you crack it, you're unstoppable! 🕵️
